name: GoCardless Bank Account Data API (Nordigen)
what_it_does: Open banking API (formerly Nordigen, acquired by GoCardless) providing PSD2-compliant access to EU bank account data — transactions, balances, and account details from 2000+ European banks via a single API.
use_cases: Aggregating EU bank account transactions for personal finance and budgeting agents
Verifying European bank account ownership for open banking-based authentication
Accessing multi-bank transaction history for credit risk assessment agents
Building PSD2-compliant account information services (AIS) in European markets
Cash flow analysis using EU bank data for financial planning applications
not_for: US bank account access — Nordigen is European open banking only
Payment initiation (use GoCardless Payments or Stripe instead)
best_when: An agent aggregates European bank account data for financial applications under PSD2 regulatory framework, accessing 2000+ EU banks from a single API.
avoid_when: You need US bank data — use Plaid or Teller instead.

auth.notes: JWT access tokens via API key and secret exchange. Tokens expire and require refresh. User authorization via bank redirect flow (similar to OAuth). EU bank authorization redirects for user consent.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 50 end user accounts/month for development
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-use: from €0.50-2.00 per connected account per month
pricing.notes: Free tier for development and small production. Pay-per-account pricing for scale. Acquired by GoCardless in 2022. Competitive with Plaid for EU markets. No minimum commitment.

performance.rate_limits: 50 requests/second; banking data freshness varies by bank

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec published. REST API well-documented for the account aggregation flow. Error responses include bank-specific error codes. Webhook delivery for requisition status changes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Account and transaction queries are idempotent reads. Requisition (bank authorization) creation is idempotent by reference.
known_agent_gotchas: EU banks only — not useful for US or non-PSD2 markets
Bank authorization via redirect is required — cannot be fully automated without user interaction
Transaction availability varies by bank — some banks have 90-day transaction history limit
GoCardless acquisition may affect API roadmap — verify current documentation currency

security_score_components.security_notes: PSD2 regulated — strong regulatory compliance framework. GDPR compliant with EU data residency. SOC2 Type II. GoCardless security standards apply post-acquisition. TLS enforced. User consent mandatory.
